what: The SAME fan-out task (classify 5 short texts) run two ways: (A) one fresh subagent per item (Claude's default fan-out), (B) one consolidated context doing all 5. Measures the coordination tax = overhead re-paid per spawn. Turns the EFFICIENCY_BENCHMARK projection into a measurement.
method: Agent-tool subagents; subagent_tokens read from each usage object. Hand-recorded from the harness.
effectiveness_note: BOTH arms produced the IDENTICAL correct labels (positive, negative, neutral, positive, negative). Same effectiveness — the difference is purely cost.

result.interpretation: For N=5 trivial items, ~80% of the fan-out cost was re-paid fixed overhead (~75,252/spawn), NOT work. The work needs the overhead paid ONCE; fan-out pays it N times.

projection_now_anchored.formula: fanout = N * 75252 ; consolidated/warm-worker = 75252 + work ; tax = (N-1) * 75252 for small items
projection_now_anchored.note: Tax fraction = overhead/(overhead+work): HIGH when items are small/coordination-heavy, LOW when each item is large reasoning.

honest_caveats: Arm B (consolidated) is cheaper but SERIALIZES the work + shares one context (no isolation) + the context grows — the tradeoff fan-out exists to avoid.

The Agix BUS is the architecture that recovers fan-out's parallelism + isolation WITHOUT re-paying per-spawn overhead (warm parallel workers, 0-token dispatch). This run measures the TAX the bus targets; it does not measure a live bus-coordinated arm (no persistent workers / SendMessage available here).
Trivial items → ~100% of fan-out cost is overhead. Large-reasoning items shrink the tax fraction.
Same model in both arms → identical effectiveness (verified: identical labels). This is a COST result, not a quality result.
